title: Rank with ties + partition + filter by rank
prompt: Write `MyApp.Shop.top_customers_with_ties/0` that returns the top 5 customers by total order amount per month, preserving ties (rank 5 can include multiple customers). Use `rank() |> over(partition_by: month, order_by: [desc: total])` — rank (not row_number) preserves ties. Use `fragment("date_trunc('month', ?)", o.placed_at)` for the month bucket. Filter the outer query to `rank <= 5`. Write to `lib/my_app/shop.ex`.
tier_rationale: Must distinguish `rank()` (ties preserved) from `row_number()` (ties broken arbitrarily) AND combine with a partition + filter — advanced understanding of window semantics.
